name: rasa.module.releases
description: Portable release-management lifecycle (track → version → tag → ship → record), mountable into any domain or orchestrator. Distilled from rasa.domain.code; the one thing that varies per project — what makes a release shippable and how it actually deploys — is delegated to a project-owned .claude/release-gate.md.

rasa.note: Engineering-scoped release module (companion to rasa.module.tasks). Ships the portable release spine — semver heuristic, the RELEASES.md timeline tracker, annotated-tag discipline, the hotfix path, dependency-audit cadence — all Element-owned. The two genuinely per-project concerns — (1) what 'shippable' requires, (2) how this project deploys — live in a project-owned .claude/release-gate.md (seeded skip-if-exists), the release analogue of module.tasks' done-gate. Cross-module references (TASK-NNN ids in the tracker, a deploy pipeline, the audit ledger) are SOFT: present-if-mounted, graceful-if-absent.

element.description: Element-owned release files mirrored into the install target's .claude/ per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. Refreshed on Element upgrade. The release-gate and the RELEASES.md tracker are NOT here — they are project-owned (see seed.files).

files.note: The portable release spine: RELEASES.md tracker format + lifecycle, version-bump heuristic, annotated-tag format, the hotfix path, dependency hygiene. Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade. References .claude/release-gate.md for the deploy command + readiness contract — it hardcodes neither.
Release skills: /release (cut a release end-to-end), /release-add (append to the next-release entry), /releases (read+render the tracker). Element-owned; refreshed on upgrade.
Describes what this module ships and how it installs. Fork/author-time doc; not installed into consumer projects.

seed.description: One-time, project-owned files copied at install only (skip-if-exists), per canon ELEMENT_CONTRACT.md §7. These belong to the consuming project after install and are never overwritten on Element upgrade — the project's release-gate and its live release tracker.

files.note: The per-project release adapter — what must be true to cut a release (tests green, build clean, audit clean, approval) AND how this project actually deploys (the deploy command / env+tag model). Project-owned; the project fills it. If rasa.module.pipelines is also mounted, the deploy command points at its 50-deploy stage. Ships with a minimum-honest default + commented examples. Placeholder-free per the documented skip-if-exists policy gap.

The live release timeline: one '🚧 Next' accumulator at the top, '✅ Shipped' entries below stamped with date+tag+sha. Project-owned; /release-add appends, /release stamps. Task-id bullets (TASK-NNN) are a soft reference to rasa.module.tasks — freeform descriptions work fine without it.

Stamped with the Element HEAD commit SHA at init time. Lockfile shape per canon Spec §6 (Connection Contract). When co-installed under a parent that also stamps a lockfile, the parent's is authoritative (single-writer-per-path, Lock L-004); in the kernel pull model each Element gets its own holding folder, so no collision. Substitutes the standard {{ELEMENT_*}} / {{TODAY}} placeholders via bin/init.

scaffold.description: Empty directories created at init by the declarative installer. The releases/ ledger ships as a seed template instead (bin/init does not implement scaffold.directories), so this stays empty.
